name: Shoyu Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pan, place chicken with soy sauce, brown sugar, star anise, ginger, sherry, and 1/2 cup water.
2. Bring to a boil, cover and simmer over a low flame, stirring occasionally to ensure that the chicken pieces are all evenly exposed to the seasoning sauce.
3. If using Chinese parsley, cook a few stalks with the chicken; otherwise, use green onions (scallions) fresh as a garnish.
4. Optionally, thicken the sauce with cornstarch by removing a tablespoon of the cooking liquid to a small bowl and stirring in about 1 teaspoon cornstarch. Mix well to dissolve. Add the mixture to the cooking liquid after the chicken has been removed and stir over medium heat until the sauce thickens. Pour over the chicken before serving.
5. Makes 4 to 6 servings.
Per serving: 581 calories, 18 gm carbohydrates, 195 mg cholesterol, : 1780 mg sodium, 64 gm protein, 27 gm fat, 7 gm saturated fat
Recipe: "Favorite Island Cookery, Book 1," published by the Honpa Hongwanji Hawaii Betsuin, a Buddhist temple in Honolulu.
